denial_pattern_sources: algo_boilerplate_fingerprint: JAMA Health Forum Feb 2024 (AI in coverage decisions); ProPublica 'Uncovered' Series 2023
ignored_physician_letter: AMA 2023 Prior Authorization Physician Survey
denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

We received your provider's request on 05/21/2026 for Letrozole ovulation induction related to Polycystic ovary syndrome with infertility (E28.2). After review, we cannot approve this request.

Our review applied UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines and Optum clinical criteria. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Letrozole ovulation induction with an out-of-network provider is not covered without a gap exception. In-network alternatives are available.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with out of network no authorization. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

You have the right to appeal this determination. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this letter. Include your member identification number and the service reference shown on your explanation of benefits.

This determination applies to your employer-sponsored commercial benefit while you remain covered under an active group plan (still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO).

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management

Standard criteria apply regardless of information previously sent to the plan.
clinical_context: The 72-year-old member has Polycystic ovary syndrome with infertility (E28.2). The member is still actively employed as a senior professional on a large-group employer-sponsored commercial PPO. She has been treated under commercial coverage with Letrozole ovulation induction recommended as the next appropriate step. Anovulatory cycles; failed clomiphene 100 mg ×3 cycles. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that out of network no authorization bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if her care is delayed further.
